name: Multi-Agent SOC — 9 Parallel AI Security Agents
description: APEX Multi-Agent SOC: 9 specialist AI agents (CVE Intel, IOC Hunter, SIEM Defender, Threat Hunter, IR Playbook, Compliance Guardian, Red Team, Zero Trust Sentinel, Risk Synthesizer) dispatched in parallel against a free-text task, with CVE/KEV/IOC context enrichment, real-time SSE streaming, and an executive risk-synthesis brief.

routes_sampled.evidence: workers/src/index.js:1955-1958 (path.startsWith prefix dispatch, agent_id read via path.split('/')[4])

frontend.evidence: frontend/soc-agents.html calls all 4 routes directly (fetch('/api/agents/stream') :427, fetch('/api/agents/run') :542, fetch('/api/agents/dispatch/...') :627,674, fetch('/api/agents/status') :699). frontend/index.html does NOT call any /api/agents/* route directly (0 matches) — it only links out to /soc-agents from the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142), mobile drawer (:1183), a homepage feature card (:5599-5609), and the flagship products grid (:5645). 6 other pages (frontend/mssp.html, frontend/god-mode.html, frontend/ai-red-team.html, frontend/soc-operations.html, frontend/attack-surface-management.html, frontend/threat-hunting.html, frontend/vulnerability-management.html) also link to /soc-agents; frontend/soc-dashboard.html and frontend/enterprise-portal.html embed only the GET /api/agents/status widget, not run/stream/dispatch.

navigation.evidence: Prominent placement in the primary desktop nav (frontend/index.html:1142, styled as a pill) and mobile drawer (:1183), plus a homepage feature card and 8 other product pages' cross-links. The /soc-agents target resolves via Cloudflare Pages' native Clean-URL lookup (frontend/_redirects, no explicit rewrite needed) to the real, present frontend/soc-agents.html — confirmed not a dead CTA.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs imports classifyTask, fetchCVEContext, fetchKEVStatus, fetchIOCContext, runAgent, handleAgentsStatus, handleAgentsRun, handleAgentDispatch, and (as of the 2026-07-08 SSE CORS fix) handleAgentsStream directly from workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js — all 9 entry_points now have real describe()-block coverage. handleAgentsStream's new 'SSE CORS' describe block asserts the 3 previously-rejected production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) are now reflected in Access-Control-Allow-Origin, plus the primary-origin and fail-closed-on-unknown-origin cases. fetchIOCContext is imported but never called in any it() block — imported, not exercised. workers/test/copilotAdvancedCapabilities.test.mjs:287-318 tests the AI-Copilot's use of this capability but does so against a full vi.doMock() of workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js, not the real module. FIXED 2026-07-13: 2 new tests in the 'handleAgentsStatus()' describe block lock in the provider-count fix below — one asserts all 6 real provider keys are present in ai_providers (not just 4), one asserts the provider_note denominator reflects Object.keys(providers).length rather than a hardcoded 4.

verification.evidence: FIXED this pass (dedicated change, own commit, own regression test — per docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md's recommendation to not bundle it into a process/documentation PR): workers/src/index.js run/stream/dispatch route blocks (~1945-1968) now call `if (!isRealUser(authCtx)) return withSecurityHeaders(withCors(unauthorized(), request));` immediately after resolveAuthV5, using the identical established pattern already in place at 30+ other routes (e.g. the MFA block immediately above it) — no new middleware, no duplicated authorization logic. status is deliberately left ungated: it is read-only registry/health data with no compute cost, embedded unauthenticated in the public SOC dashboard widget (frontend/soc-dashboard.html:539, frontend/enterprise-portal.html), and both frontend consumers of the gated routes (frontend/soc-agents.html) already shipped with dormant 401-handling (handleUnauth(), :387-393, and per-call `if (resp.status===401)` checks at :434,544,628,675) that had never been exercised until now — this fix requires zero frontend changes. Regression coverage added: workers/test/authGateRealUser.test.mjs now asserts anonymous POST /api/agents/{run,stream,dispatch/:id} → 401, admin key → not-401 for run and dispatch, and anonymous GET /api/agents/status stays non-401. Full suite re-run green: 177 files / 1862 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-08). SECOND FIX, same day, own commit: the SSE CORS finding (item 1 in notes, below) is now also fixed — workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js's handleAgentsStream now imports `corsHeaders` from workers/src/middleware/cors.js and uses it in place of the hand-rolled `origin.endsWith('.cyberdudebivash.in')` check, so the 3 previously-rejected real production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) now get SSE streaming. New regression coverage: workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs's 'handleAgentsStream() — SSE CORS' describe block (5 cases: the 3 previously-broken origins now reflected, the primary origin still reflected, an unrecognized origin still fails closed to the primary origin). Full suite re-run green: 177 files / 1867 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-08). THIRD FIX, 2026-07-13, own commit: the provider-count undercounting finding (notes, below) is now also fixed — handleAgentsStatus (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js) now checks all 6 real provider env keys (added together/TOGETHER_API_KEY and anthropic/ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, confirmed against workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js's PROVIDER_CONFIG) and the provider_note denominator is now Object.keys(providers).length instead of a hardcoded 4, so it can never drift again as providers are added. New regression coverage: 2 tests in workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs's 'handleAgentsStatus()' describe block. Full suite re-run green: 274 files / 2862 tests (npx vitest run, 2026-07-13). operational_status remains NOT READY — this fix closes only the cosmetic/minor finding; the two still-open items below (agent-selection defaults, missing subscription gate on the direct routes) are unchanged and were explicitly judged to require a product decision, not a code fix, so they were deliberately not touched in this pass. Prior static-verification evidence for the original findings retained below for audit trail.

notes: FIXED 2026-07-08 (see verification.evidence for the fix). Original finding, retained verbatim for audit trail: Highest-value finding: none of the 4 MASOC routes gate on isRealUser(authCtx) or check authCtx.authenticated before running — resolveAuthV5's IP-fallback branch sets authenticated:true for every anonymous caller (workers/src/auth/middleware.js's own comment: 'authenticated: true means not-an-invalid-API-key, NOT logged in'), and 30+ other routes in workers/src/index.js do gate on isRealUser (e.g. :2054,3358,4050) including the MFA block immediately above this one (:1917). The only real control on these 4 routes is a 5-runs/min KV rate limiter keyed by user_id/key_id/IP (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js:176-199, fail-closed if KV is unavailable) — meaning anyone, unauthenticated, can invoke compute-expensive parallel AI-agent orchestration. This directly contradicts a SEPARATE, correctly-gated path to the same capability: workers/src/handlers/aiSecurityCopilot.js:1008,1861-1868,2718 exposes the same functionality as a Copilot tool restricted to PRO/TEAM/ENTERPRISE/MSSP tiers, actively enforced. subscription_gated is recorded as false here because it describes THIS capability's own 4 direct routes; run/stream/dispatch now require a real logged-in principal but still carry no tier/subscription gate of their own (unlike the Copilot-mediated path) — that remains a separate, unfixed gap, not conflated with the auth-gate fix. Two further findings were identified, one now also fixed, one still open: (1) FIXED 2026-07-08, same day as the auth-gate fix, own commit: handleAgentsStream (workers/src/handlers/multiAgentSOC.js) hand-rolled its own CORS check instead of using the shared corsHeaders()/withCors() the other 3 routes use, and its check was narrower than the real 6-origin PROD_ORIGINS allowlist (workers/src/middleware/cors.js) — 3 of 6 valid production origins (cyberdudebivash.pages.dev, tools.cyberdudebivash.com, intel.cyberdudebivash.com) had SSE streaming silently rejected by the browser while the JSON/status routes kept working fine from the same origin. Now uses corsHeaders(request, env) directly — see verification.evidence for the fix and its regression coverage. (2) Still open, out of scope for both fixes above (frontend state logic, judged too architecturally consequential to change without confirming intent): frontend/soc-agents.html defaults to selecting all 9 agents including risk_synthesizer (:226-236,269) and both its run paths always send a non-empty agents array (:403,528), so the page's own label text ('all selected = auto-routing by task classifier', :157) is unreachable — classifyTask() is never invoked from this page — and because risk_synthesizer runs once standalone inside the parallel batch (with only CVE/KEV/IOC context, contradicting its own system prompt's expectation of other agents' findings) AND again correctly via the separate synthesis step, every default run burns an extra AI call on a low-value duplicate card. FIXED 2026-07-13: Minor/cosmetic: handleAgentsStatus (:697-702) checked only 4 of the 6 env keys workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js actually routes through (missing TOGETHER_API_KEY, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), understating the 'X/4 providers active' stat — Groq/DeepSeek/CF AI (the 3 the UI copy names) are all genuinely called via real HTTP requests, confirmed in workers/src/core/aiProviderRouter.js. Now checks all 6 with a dynamic denominator — see verification.evidence for the fix. FIXED 2026-07-12 (Tier-3 backlog item #5, full-frontend-audit follow-up): frontend/soc-agents.html's per-agent meta row read `result.tokens?.total_tokens` — a field that has never existed on any of the 3 routes' real responses. runAgent() passes routeAICall()'s return value through unchanged, and routeAICall()/dispatchToProvider() (src/core/aiProviderRouter.js) only ever produce `tokens: { input, output }` — so the 🔢 token-count pill silently never rendered, for any agent, on any successful run, regardless of real usage. Fixed to sum `tokens.input + tokens.output`. Also corrected a stale mock in workers/test/multiAgentSOC.test.mjs that mirrored the same wrong `total_tokens` shape (harmless to that file's own assertions, which only checked property presence, but a misleading model of the real contract left in place); replaced with the real `{ input, output }` shape. New workers/test/socAgentsTokenCountFix.test.mjs (3 tests, static parse); test_coverage.has_tests above unchanged (frontend-only fix, no new backend entry_point coverage).

name: Autonomous Defense Engine (SAFE / ASSISTED / AGGRESSIVE auto-response)
description: 3-tier automated threat-response engine: SAFE (recommendations only), ASSISTED (queue for 1-click human approval), AGGRESSIVE (fully automatic detect → generate → deploy → alert, no human required). Configurable CVSS/KEV/active-exploitation thresholds and a per-run auto-deploy cap; deploys to splunk/elastic/sentinel/generic_webhook target platforms; maintains an execution log and rollback-within-window capability.

frontend.evidence: frontend/index.html's #auto-defense section (data-auth-gate="true", ~71 lines): mode selector (cdbDefenseSetMode), threshold config form (cdbDefenseSaveConfig), execution log + pending-approvals queue (cdbDefenseLoadExecutions/cdbDefenseLoadPending), approve/rollback actions — all via real fetch() calls to the 8 routes above, confirmed 2026-07-11 while investigating a customer report that several homepage dashboard sections 'aren't properly built.'

navigation.evidence: CORRECTED 2026-07-11, same day: this field originally claimed 'no nav link existed anywhere for this section' — independently re-verified false (customer reported the fix hadn't changed anything visible, prompting a re-check). A pre-existing p3InjectNav() (frontend/index.html's Phase 3 Engine block) already injected a real nav link labeled 'Defense' for this exact section — a static grep never found it because the link is built via document.createElement()/property assignment, not literal href="..." text. The real, now-fixed bugs: p3InjectNav() ran unconditionally for every visitor, including logged-out ones who can never pass this section's own auth gate (a nav item that always dead-ends for the one visitor who can see it), and it only ever targeted the desktop nav, leaving mobile with zero discoverability. Fixed: p3InjectNav() now gates on window.CDB_AUTH.isAuthenticated and injects into both desktop nav and the mobile drawer; a redundant, differently-labeled ('Auto-Defense') injection this entry originally described was removed from cdbApplyGates() — it would have shown this section twice, under two different names, for an authenticated visitor. CDB_SECTIONS now carries the real, already-shipped 'Defense' label (matching p3InjectNav(), not inventing a new one) instead of falling back to the raw section id.

test_coverage.evidence: Partial. workers/test/defenseEnginePosture.test.mjs imports handleGetDefensePosture directly (5 tests: real field aliasing/counting, honest null when no data). workers/test/authGateRealUser.test.mjs now covers the auth gate for all 8 routes (anonymous → 401, admin key → non-401, 2026-07-11). The core business logic of handleSetDefenseMode/handleExecuteDefense/handleGetPending/handleApprove/handleRollback/handleGetExecutions has no dedicated test coverage of its own — a real gap, not fixed in this pass.

verification.evidence: Discovered uncatalogued (zero registry entry anywhere despite 8 live, real routes) while investigating a customer report about under-built homepage sections. Read workers/src/handlers/autoDefenseEngine.js in full (444 lines) and every one of its 8 route registrations in workers/src/index.js directly — none checked isRealUser(authCtx) or any tier/role, the same vulnerability class already fixed once for this domain's sibling (CAP-MASOC-001), except worse: an unauthenticated caller could change the platform's live defense mode, fabricate a 'threat' object to trigger a real AGGRESSIVE-mode auto-deploy to real target platforms, or approve/rollback a real pending action — not merely a compute-abuse risk. Fixed in the same pass: all 8 routes (including the read-only GETs — unlike CAP-MASOC-001's status route, no route here has a legitimate anonymous caller, since the only frontend consumer is itself data-auth-gate="true") now gate on isRealUser(authCtx), same established pattern used 30+ times elsewhere in workers/src/index.js. Regression coverage: workers/test/authGateRealUser.test.mjs, 2 new describe blocks (8 new 'anonymous → 401' cases, 5 new 'admin key passes' cases, plus 2 explicit fabricated-threat/mode-change rejection tests). Also fixed: the handler's own header docblock said routes were /api/defense/* — a stale mismatch against the real, live /api/defense-engine/* prefix actually wired in workers/src/index.js — corrected to match reality. Full suite green: 217 files / 2247+ tests (exact count in this wave's PR).

notes: The frontend/index.html section this capability powers was itself invisible to any visitor who didn't already know the exact #auto-defense URL prior to this same wave's navigation fix (see navigation.evidence) — combined with the auth-gate finding above, this capability was effectively unreachable through any legitimate path AND simultaneously callable by anyone who found the raw API path. Both are now fixed. Residual, not fixed here: (1) core business-logic test coverage gap noted in test_coverage.evidence; (2) mssp_command / enterprise_shield-style tier restriction was deliberately NOT added — this capability requires only isRealUser(authCtx), no tier/subscription gate, matching how the section is presented in the UI (no plan-gating copy, no upsell prompt) — if a tier restriction is actually intended, that's a product decision to make explicitly, not inferred here.
